theme.designMd:
# Design System: High-Performance Athletic Interface

## 1. Overview & Creative North Star
### Creative North Star: "The Kinetic Lab"
This design system is engineered for the elite athlete. It rejects the soft, "lifestyle" aesthetics of generic wellness apps in favor of a high-precision, data-heavy environment that mirrors a professional performance lab. 

The design breaks the traditional "box-and-line" template through intentional density and asymmetric rhythm. We prioritize rapid data ingestion and analysis. By overlapping modular components and utilizing high-contrast typography, we create an interface that feels like a calibrated instrument: serious, disciplined, and uncompromising.

## 2. Colors: High-Contrast Performance
The palette is built on an absolute obsidian foundation (#0e0e0e), allowing performance metrics to glow with urgency.

Primary (#ff8f6f / #ff734a): Performance Orange. Used for primary calls to action and critical success metrics.
Secondary (#00e3fd): Electric Blue. Dedicated to data visualization, active tracking states, and secondary highlights.
Tertiary (#c2ff99): Limbic Green. Reserved for optimal performance zones and completed goals.

The No-Line Rule: use tonal shifts, negative space, and subtle gradients instead of 1px borders.
Glassmorphism: floating headers or sticky navigation should use semi-transparent surface color with 20px blur.

## 3. Typography: The Editorial Edge
Display and headlines use Space Grotesk.
Body and labels use Inter.
Use display-lg for daily calorie totals and headline-sm for meal titles.

## 4. Elevation & Depth: Tonal Layering
Base: surface (#0e0e0e)
Sectioning: surface-container-low (#131313)
Interactive Cards: surface-container (#1a1a1a)
Pop-overs/Modals: surface-bright (#2c2c2c)
Use tinted glows instead of black shadows.

## 5. Components: Engineered Primitives
Cards and meal tracking forbid divider lines.
Buttons: solid primary, ghost secondary, dense action chips.
Input fields: minimalist, bottom-only underline, technical emphasis on numeric values.
Custom gauge: semicircular arc with primary-to-tertiary gradient.

## 6. Do's and Don'ts
Do prioritize data density and use Space Grotesk for hero numbers.
Don't use large radii, cute empty states, pure white body text, or standard drop shadows.
